Why BMIIL

The right place
at the right time

India's healthcare innovation is concentrated in 5 cities. Tier-2 India — home to 800 million people — has no medtech infrastructure. BMIIL is built to change that, starting from Bareilly with a model designed to replicate across North India.

Our Edge

Six reasons to build
your startup here

01

Tier-2 India Advantage

Lower burn, massive patient access, authentic problem statements from an underserved healthcare market that investors are increasingly watching.

02

Captive Clinical Pipeline

Direct access to BIU's teaching hospital gives startups immediate problem identification, validation partners, and pilot environments.

03

Regulatory Navigation

In-house mentors experienced in CDSCO, FDA, AYUSH, and WHO pathways — reducing time-to-compliance by months.

04

The Ayurveda Angle

No other medtech incubator in India offers a credible, evidence-based Ayurveda + technology fusion track backed by university research.

05

Grant Ecosystem Access

Active pipeline to DST, BIRAC, Startup India, and international grants — our team actively facilitates applications and submissions.

06

Multidisciplinary Faculty

200+ researchers across Medicine, Pharmacy, Biotech, Dental and Allied Health available as subject matter experts and co-innovators.

Home/About
Our Story & Mission

We exist because
800 million people
deserve better.

India's medtech innovation ecosystem is clustered in 5 cities and serves a fraction of the country. BMIIL was founded on a single conviction: that the next wave of healthcare breakthroughs won't come from a metro co-working space — they'll come from people who live with the problem every day.

BMIIL
"The most important medical innovations of the next decade will be built by clinicians and engineers who understand what it means to live in Tier-2 India — not just visit it."
— BMIIL Founding Principle

Incubate

Turn clinical insights and research into validated, fundable healthcare companies

Validate

Give startups the clinical access, lab infrastructure, and regulatory expertise to prove their solutions work

Scale

Connect innovators to investors, hospital networks, and global markets — from Bareilly to the world

Our Story

The gap that
demanded
a response.

Uttar Pradesh is home to over 240 million people — more than Brazil. It has one of the highest burdens of preventable disease in South Asia. And until BMIIL, it had no dedicated medtech incubation infrastructure of any kind.

The founding idea was simple and urgent: Bareilly International University already has the hospitals, the labs, the students, and the faculty. What it lacked was a structured system to convert clinical observations into startups.

"Every day in a public hospital, doctors encounter problems that they know could be solved — if someone would just build the right tool. We are that someone."

BMIIL was built to be that bridge. Not a generic startup hub with a healthcare vertical — but a deeply clinical institution where the problem always comes before the product, and the patient always comes before the pitch.

Situated within BIU's campus in Bareilly, we have direct access to a multi-speciality teaching hospital, specialised research laboratories, and a multidisciplinary faculty — giving our startups infrastructure that most metro incubators cannot offer at any price.

Journey So Far

✓
2024 — Foundation
The Idea Takes Shape

Dr. Varun Agarwal and Claudius Lazarus identify the gap in Tier-2 India's healthcare innovation infrastructure. Initial conversations with Bareilly International University begin.

✓
2025 — Establishment
BMIIL is Founded

Entity incorporated. Hosting agreement signed with BIU. First grant applications submitted. Core team begins forming around the founding vision.

✓
2025–26 — Validation
Institutional Backing Secured

First grant approvals received. Letters of intent signed with key partners. Mentor network assembled. Infrastructure setup underway on BIU campus.

→
2026 — Launch
Cohort 1 Opens

First cohort of 10–12 startups across Medtech Incubation, Accelerator, and Ayush tracks. Demo Day and first investor introductions planned for end of year.

→
2027 — Scale
Replication Begins

Second cohort launches. Framework for replicating the BMIIL model with partner universities across North India.

Our Institutional Home

Bareilly International
University — more than
a host campus.

BIU isn't a landlord — it's a co-creator. The University's clinical infrastructure, research faculty, and student talent pool are actively integrated into BMIIL's program delivery.

🏥

Multi-Speciality Teaching Hospital

500+ bed hospital with OPD, IPD, and surgical facilities — directly accessible to BMIIL startups for problem identification and pilot testing.

⚗️

12+ Specialised Research Labs

Pharmacy, biotech, clinical pathology, dental, and allied health science labs — available for R&D, prototype testing, and formulation development.

👩‍🔬

200+ Research Faculty

Multidisciplinary academics across Medicine, Ayurveda, Pharmacy, Nursing, Dental, and Allied Health — available as subject matter experts and mentors.

🎓

Talent Pipeline

Thousands of students across health science disciplines — a direct pool of future co-founders, clinical advisors, and early team members.

What We Stand For

The principles that
guide every decision.

01

Problem First, Always

We don't incubate technology — we incubate solutions to real clinical problems. Every startup that joins BMIIL must begin with a deeply understood, patient-validated problem statement. No exceptions.

02

Rigor Over Hype

Healthcare is not a place for shortcuts. We demand clinical evidence, regulatory thinking, and intellectual honesty from every team we work with — and we model the same ourselves.

03

Tier-2 as a Strength

We don't apologise for being in Bareilly. We celebrate it. Lower costs, authentic problem exposure, and a healthcare market underserved for too long — this is an advantage, not a compromise.

04

Traditional Knowledge, Modern Evidence

India's Ayurvedic heritage is a globally unique innovation asset. We treat it with scientific seriousness — validating, protecting, and building on it rather than dismissing or romanticising it.

05

Founders Come First

Our terms are founder-friendly. Our incentive is your success. We measure ourselves by the outcomes of our portfolio companies, not by the number of startups we admit.

06

Build to Last

We are not building a programme. We are building an institution — one designed to outlast any individual, any cohort, and any funding cycle.

The Bigger Picture

BMIIL isn't just
a centre — it's a
replicable model.

India has 800+ universities outside its metro cities. The majority sit adjacent to district hospitals, have pharmacy and biotech facilities, and are surrounded by communities with unmet healthcare needs.

BMIIL's Bareilly unit is designed to prove — rigorously and publicly — that a clinically-grounded medtech incubator can be built, funded, and sustained in Tier-2 India.

The long-term vision is a national network of BMIIL-model centres, each anchored to a university with clinical infrastructure, each generating a pipeline of locally relevant healthcare startups.

2026

Bareilly — Proof of Concept

First cohort. First validated startups. First exits or funding rounds.

2027

North India Expansion

Two additional BMIIL centres in partner universities across UP and Uttarakhand.

2028

National Medtech Network

A federated network of 5+ BMIIL-model centres contributing to a shared national deal flow.

2030

Global Medtech Hub

BMIIL-model centres contributing to India's position as a global medtech innovation hub.

800M+underserved patients

In Tier-2 and Tier-3 India — the largest untapped healthcare market in the world.

₹9L Crhealthcare market

India's total healthcare market, majority concentrated in 8 metros today.

10–12startups per cohort

Selected for depth of impact, clinical rigor, and team quality — not volume.

3incubation tracks

Medtech, Accelerator, and Ayush — covering the full spectrum of healthcare innovation.

12 monthsprogramme duration

Structured phases from problem validation through regulatory readiness to investor introduction.

The Opportunity

The largest gap in
Indian healthcare.

$372B
India Healthcare Market by 2030

Growing at 22% CAGR — driven largely by Tier-2 and rural demand

800M+
People in Underserved Markets

Outside India's top-8 cities with limited access to modern medtech solutions

<5%
Medtech Startups from Tier-2

Despite this market representing the majority of India's healthcare burden

3×
Lower Burn vs Metro Incubators

Bareilly's cost structure gives startups more runway for the same investment

Why Now

Why now is the moment
to back Tier-2 medtech.

01

Government tailwinds.

PLI for medtech, Ayushman Bharat, National Digital Health Mission — all driving capital and attention toward underserved healthcare markets.

02

University infrastructure, de-risked.

BIU's hospital, labs, and faculty are already in place. We're not building from scratch — we're activating what exists.

03

Ayurveda as a global differentiator.

The ₹1.2 lakh crore global herbal and nutraceutical market has no credible scientifically-validated Indian pipeline. BMIIL's Ayush track is built to supply one.

04

First-mover in a white space.

No comparable medtech incubator exists in Uttar Pradesh, India's most populous state. The addressable market is uncrowded and large.

Participation Models

How you can partner with BMIIL.

Angel & VC Investment

Financial Partner

Direct equity participation in BMIIL's fund structure or co-investment rights into individual portfolio startups at pre-negotiated terms.

→ Preferred co-investment access
→ Quarterly portfolio updates
→ Demo day priority seating
→ Advisory board participation
→ Deal flow from BIU research pipeline
Corporate & Hospital Partner

Strategic Partner

Gain privileged access to BMIIL's innovation pipeline — with rights to pilot, license, or acquire technologies developed within the incubator.

→ First-look licensing rights
→ Dedicated problem statement pipeline
→ Named co-innovation track
→ Joint IP development options
→ Clinical trial facilitation
Government & Grant Body

Institutional Partner

We actively pursue and facilitate DST, BIRAC, Startup India, and international grant pipelines for our portfolio companies and for BMIIL itself.

→ Active grant pipeline management
→ BIRAC & DST applications
→ Startup India recognition
→ International research partnerships
→ Policy engagement & representation
